_comment: Character manifest for the illustrated B-roll system (character_assets.py). Additive: this only supplements the existing Pexels stock-footage path, never replaces it. To add a new character later: add an entry here + drop matching image files under ASSETS_ROOT/Characters/<folder_name>/{Expressions,Poses,Reference,Scenes}/ - zero code changes required.
_available_assets_note: available_assets below is a STATIC declared list of the asset subfolders/kinds each character is expected to have (not a scan of the real folder contents). Tradeoff: static keeps the manifest human-editable and diffable in code review, and lets select_character_asset() know what kinds of assets *should* exist without touching disk just to answer 'does this character have poses'. The actual filename picked for a given beat is still resolved at runtime by scanning ASSETS_ROOT/Characters/<folder_name>/<asset_type>/ on disk (see character_assets.py), so this list never goes stale in a way that breaks anything - it's just a hint, not a cache.
